title: Remediation Tools — Extended Documentation
content:
Remediation tools modify system state and require careful use.

restart_execution: Restarts a failed execution from its last checkpoint. Safe for most workflows. Requires workflow_name and optionally execution_id. If execution_id is omitted, restarts the latest failed execution. Check MAX_RESTARTS_PER_WORKFLOW limit before calling.

trigger_workflow: Triggers a fresh execution of a workflow. Use when restart is not appropriate (e.g., the previous execution completed but produced wrong output). Requires workflow_name. Optional parameters dict for workflow inputs.

requeue_item: Requeues a single failed item from a processing queue. Use for item-level failures in batch workflows. Requires queue_name and item_id.

bulk_retry_failures: Retries ALL failed executions in a time window. HIGH RISK — requires approval. Use only when multiple workflows failed due to a transient issue (e.g., database was down, now recovered). Check MAX_BULK_OPERATIONS limit.

disable_workflow: Disables a workflow's schedule. HIGH RISK — requires approval. Use only to prevent further damage while investigating. Remember to re-enable after resolution.

Never use remediation tools on PROTECTED_WORKFLOWS without explicit human approval.
